titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Brooklyn Historical Society newspaper collection ARC.258
titlestmt.author: John Zarrillo

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-24 20:23:01 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

change.item: Revised by Dee Bowers to reflect rehousing
Finding aid rearranged by Dee Bowers

physdesc.extent: in 59 boxes, 252 bound volumes, and 87 reels of microfilm

did.abstract: Newspapers primarily published in Brooklyn dating from the 18th to 21st centuries. Also includes a selection of newspapers published in Queens, Long Island, upstate New York, and New Jersey. In addition to paper editions, the collection includes microfilm copies of certain publications.
did.langmaterial: Materials in English, Dutch, Finnish, Hebrew, Italian, Latvian, Lithuanian, Norwegian, Polish, Russian, and Spanish.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The newspapers were found in collection, with the exception of the March 1, 1873 issue of the Kings County Gazette, which was the gift of Martin Fishman, 2001.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Many of these titles are available via Brooklyn Newsstand, CBH's online database of digitized Brooklyn newspapers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The newspapers are arranged alphabetically by title.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Brooklyn Historical Society newspaper collection, ARC.258, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was assembled from uncataloged newspapers found in Brooklyn Historical Society's library and archives.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Brooklyn Newsstand, CBH's online database of digitized Brooklyn newspapers
Local newspapers on microfilm collection BCMS.0028
Brooklyn high school newspaper collection BCMS.0030
Chandos Fulton notes on the history of Brooklyn newspapers 1973.223
Thomas N. Schroth and Raymond A. Schroth, S.J. Brooklyn Eagle collection ARC.093
Brooklyn neighborhood associations and civic organizations publications ARC.167
Elizabeth Feldhusen collection of Brooklyn ephemera ARC.299
World War I political cartoons collection CBHM.0011
Long Island Weekly Intelligencer subscription receipt 1977.625
New York Daily News strike collection 1990.025

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection is primarily composed of newspapers published in Brooklyn, New York, dating from the 18th century to the 21st. In addition to paper editions, the collection includes microfilm copies of certain publications.

Geographically, the newspapers cover the numerous neighborhoods of Brooklyn, as well the other boroughs of New York City (Manhattan, Queens, Staten Island, and the Bronx), Long Island (Suffolk and Nassau Counties), and parts of upstate New York and New Jersey. The collection also includes issues of The Liberator, an abolitionist newspaper which was published in Boston, Massachusetts.

Some newspapers were published for specific communities (African-Americans, Caribbean-Americans, Catholics, Jewish-Americans, the military) or specific topics (business, real estate, antiques, computers).
One newspaper of note is The Christian Union, which was edited by Henry Ward Beecher.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: The Linewaiters' Gazette July 1999 issue in this collection was moved to the Linewaiters' Gazette Collection (BCMS.0027).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.

controlaccess.corpname: United States Maritime Service. Sheepshead Bay Training Station

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: African-American community newspaper. July 3 - August 14, 1999; June 30, 2007.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Serving Brooklyn, Queens, and Long Island." Missing September 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covering Atlantic Avenue and surrounding communities Boerum Hill, Brooklyn Heights, Cobble Hill, Downtown Brooklyn and Fort Greene.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Bohack's grocery store. Pages 3 and 4 only. November 1951 - January 1957.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Researchers should use the digitized version of this publication via Brooklyn Newsstand whenever possible to limit handling of the fragile originals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Serving Downtown Brooklyn, Clinton Hill, Fort Greene, Bedford-Stuyvesant, Prospect Heights, Crown Heights, Wingate, East Flatbush, Brownsville, Bushwick, Starrett City." September 28, 1998; February 8, 1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Serving Gowanus, Park Slope, Prospect Heights, Windsor Terrace, Brooklyn Heights, DUMBO, Metrotech, Boerum Hill, Carroll Garderns, Cobble Hill, Red Hook, Williamsburg, and Greenpoint." This issue concerns Hurricane Sandy.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Researchers should use the digitized version of this publication via Brooklyn Newsstand whenever possible to limit handling of the fragile originals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two bound volumes, each July through December of one year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: April 25, September 21, 1861; March 28, April 1, June 23, October 6, October 7, 1862.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sections include Brooklyn Planning, Industrial, Long Island Planning, and Outdoor Life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Feb. 20, November 9, 17, 1998; Feb. 16, 1999; July 1 - August 23, 1999; April 27, 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: January-June issues include April 21, 2005 issue of Brooklyn Eagle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: January-February; March 1 - June 15; June 16 - September 29; October-December.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: January 1-24; February 1 - April 30 (including April 5 issue of Brooklyn Eagle); May 1 - July 31 (including May 10, May 24, June 21 issues of Brooklyn Eagle); August 1 - October 31 (including August 16 and September 20 issues of Brooklyn Eagle); November-December (including December 20 issue of Brooklyn Eagle).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: March 1 - May 30; June 2 - August 28; September 2 - October 31.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: January 3 – June 30, 1859; January 2, 1861 – June 30, 1865; January 2 – December 31, 1866; July 1 – December 31, 1867; January 2, 1868 – June 29, 1872; January 2, 1873 – August 30, 1893; January 2, 1894 – December 30, 1911.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Researchers should use the digitized version of this publication via Brooklyn Newsstand whenever possible to limit handling of the fragile originals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One bound volume. Includes letter from publishers and list of original subscribers. September 14, 1863 - September 13, 1864.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not a complete run. Covering Downtown Brooklyn, Fort Greene, Clinton Hill, Brooklyn Heights, DUMBO, Prospect Heights, and Boerum Hill. February 17 - August 25; September 22 - December.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covers Brooklyn Heights and Downtown Brooklyn. October 8-21, 1990; July 12 - August 16, 1999; April 24, 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not a complete run. January 1999 - November 2004.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: Continued by Brooklyn Heights Press and Cobble Hill News.

did.unittitle: Brooklyn Heights Press and Cobble Hill News

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 5, 1969; April 29, July 1 - August 13, 1999; April 27, 2000; October 23, 2003; August 15, 2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The monthly business section of The Phoenix. July and October issues.

did.unittitle: The Brooklyn Journal (of Arts and Urban Affairs)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not a complete run. Includes Cobble Hill, Carroll Gardens, and Downtown Brooklyn editions. 1983-1988; October 2003-February 2006.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Park Slope, Brooklyn Heights, Carroll Gardens, Cobble Hill, Boerum Hill."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Previously published as Downtown Brooklyn (1974-1976) by the Brooklyn Chamber of Commerce and Downtown Brooklyn Development Corporation. Not a complete run.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not a complete run. July 1999 - January 2006; September 2015; September-December 2016.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 24 - December 30, 1983; December 8, 1995; March 29 - November 22, 1996; July 23 - August 13, 1999, May-June 2002.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by the Flatbush Tenants Council. "Servicing Flatbush, Midwood, Kings Highway, Gravesend and Sheepshead Bay."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covers "Canarsie, Marine Park, Mill Basin, Georgetown, Gerritsen Beach, Bergen Beach, Kings Plaza, Sheepheads Bay, Flatlands, Flatbush, East New York, and Spring Creek."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: August-December 1983; July 22 - August 12, 1999; November 7, 2002; October 30, 2003 - March 26, 2004.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not a complete run. April 5, 2012 - July 13, 2013; August - November 2015; September 2016 - March 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sag Harbor, New York. May 7, 1921 – January 1942.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by the Park Slope Civic Council. Nineteen bound volumes.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: Published by the Southern Brooklyn Community Organization and the Ocean Parkway Community Development Corporation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Long Island City, New York. Not a complete run.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: Becomes Brooklyn Progress. Published by the Downtown Brooklyn Development Association.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: Published by the East New York Development Corporation.

did.physdesc: Some of the papers are in fragile condition and some have clippings cut out of the pages.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: Published in Blythebourne, today the Borough Park neighborhood of Brooklyn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covers the Latino community of Williamsburg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not a complete run. October 11, 1866 - September 9, 1925.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Published by and for the men of the Sheepshead Bay Training Station."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covers Bay Ridge and Sunset Park. April - July 1998; July 2 - August 13, 1999.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Fragile. Researchers should use other formats if possible.

did.unittitle: Kings County Rural and Brighton Gazette

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jamaica, New York. Not a complete run. 1821-1832; 1873-1900.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted. Unavailable to researchers due to fragile condition.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Researchers should use the digitized version on Brooklyn Newsstand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One bound volume. Not a complete run.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flushing, New York. January 3, 1861 – December 27, 1877; November 7, 1878 – December 24, 1885; April 4, 1896.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Long Island City, New York. January 2, 1880 – August 4, 1882; January 4, 1884 – December 25, 1896; January 4, 1901 – December 28, 1906; 1916.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Greater New York military community. Four bound volumes.

did.unittitle: New York Mail and Express supplement - Illustrated Saturday Magazine
New-York Packet and the American Advertiser

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covers Carroll Gardens and Cobble Hill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "El periodico del la communidad Catolica por The Tablet" (Catholic periodical).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: African-American businesses and customers. March 1996; May 2004; June 16, 2007.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Covers Park Slope, Windsor Terrace, Prospect Heights, and Gowanus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: August 4-17, 1994; May 31, June 14, August 9, 16, 1999; January 28 - February 3, 2011; December 9-15, 2016.

did.unittitle: The Park Slope Paper - Windsor Terrace Edition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paid advertisement by the Flatbush Development Corp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Section 2 only. See also: Brooklyn, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Brownstone Brooklyn." March 25, August 2, 1996; May 15, May 29, June 5, November 13, 1998.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rockville Centre, New York. July 7, 1865 – June 12, 1868; June 25, 1869 – June 10, 1870.

did.unittitle: The Political Intelligencer and New-Jersey Advertiser

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not a complete run. August 6, 1892 - December 25, 1931.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hempstead, New York. June 3, 1858 – May 26, 1898; June 1, 1899 – May 25, 1922.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Greenport, New York. 1876 issues are very fragile. Not a complete run.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Far Rockaway, New York. February 11, 1903 – January 14, 1905.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A neighborhood magazine of shops, services, previews, reviews and lifestyles."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rockville Centre, New York. February 1882 – November 1920.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: East New York. Not a complete run.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Huntington, New York. January 4, 1918 – December 26, 1919; January 6, 1922 – December 28, 1923; January 2, 1925 – December 28, 1927.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not a complete run. January 3, 1851 - November 20, 1857.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roman Catholic Diocese of Brooklyn. Also includes Queens. January 1 - December 24, 1983; July 3 - August 14, 1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding Holland Society of New York.

did.unittitle: Whip and Satirist of New-York and Brooklyn
